pattern: A sliding window whose right edge always advances, and whose left edge only advances when the newest value's count would exceed the cap, tracks the longest stretch where every value stays within its allowance.
hints: Think about growing a window from left to right and keeping a running count of how many times each value currently appears inside it.
Whenever including the new right-hand value would push its own count above the limit, shrink the window from the left one step at a time until that value is back within the limit.

Because only the value you just added can ever be over the limit (everything else was fine one step ago), you never need to re-check the whole window -- just track the best window length seen so far after each shrink.

edge_inputs.description: repeating three-value pattern with a moderate cap
two alternating values with cap one
single repeated value with cap below its total count
cap equal to array length allows the whole array
all distinct values, any cap of one or more allows the full array
one value far over cap forces a strict shrink around it
large uniform value at the upper bound of the value range

stress.description: large array drawn from a small alphabet so frequencies climb quickly, cap scaled with size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    alphabet = max(1, n // 50)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, alphabet) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: small alphabet relative to length keeps frequencies interesting for the shrink logic
